'''Matty Ashton''' (born 28 July 1998) is an English [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|wg}}er for the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]] and {{rlnt|England}} at international level. 

He previously played for [[Swinton Lions]], where he made his professional debut in 2019. He made his debut at international level for England in 2023.

==Early life==
Ashton grew up in [[Heywood, Greater Manchester]], and began playing [[rugby league]] at the age of seven with [[Rochdale Mayfield]]. He later attended [[Hopwood Hall College]], where he was coached by [[Matt Calland]].<ref name="Ashton hoping to make his mark">{{cite web |title=Ashton hoping to make his mark |url=https://www.totalrl.com/ashton-hoping-to-make-his-mark/ |website=Total Rugby League |access-date=26 July 2023 |date=2 December 2019}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Early career===
After playing for two seasons in Rochdale Mayfield's open age team in the [[National Conference League]], Ashton spent a year in Australia, where he played for the [[Mullumbimby Giants]] in the [[Northern Rivers Regional Rugby League]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Craig |first=Mitchell |date=June 25, 2018 |title=Matt is magic for Mullumbimby in NRRRL |quote=MULLUMBIMBY fullback scores four tries in emphatic win over Lower Clarence in NRRRL |url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/news/nsw/lismore/sport/matt-is-magic-for-mullumbimby-in-nrrrl/news-story/19e44f03a58f806bcc408a697627407c |url-access=subscription}}</ref><ref name="Former Mayfield player wins Betfred Championship Young Player of the Year">{{cite web |title=Former Mayfield player wins Betfred Championship Young Player of the Year |url=https://www.rochdaleonline.co.uk/news-features/4/sport-news/130492/former-mayfield-player-wins-betfred-championship-young-player-of-the-year |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210127071034/https://www.rochdaleonline.co.uk/news-features/4/sport-news/130492/former-mayfield-player-wins-betfred-championship-young-player-of-the-year |url-status=usurped |archive-date=January 27, 2021 |website=Rochdale Online |access-date=26 July 2023 |date=1 October 2019}}</ref>

After a successful trial, Ashton signed a one-year contract with [[RFL Championship|Championship]] side [[Swinton Lions]] in December 2018.<ref>{{cite web |title=Lions Sign Duo Warne And Ashton For 2019 Season |url=https://swintonlionsrlfc.co.uk/news/lions-sign-duo-warne-and-ashton-for-2019-season/ |website=Swinton Lions |access-date=26 July 2023 |date=11 December 2018}}</ref> He became the league's top try scorer during the 2019 season, scoring 30 tries in total,<ref name="Ashton hoping to make his mark" /> and won the Championship Young Player of the Year award.<ref name="Former Mayfield player wins Betfred Championship Young Player of the Year" />

===Warrington Wolves===
In August 2019, Ashton was signed by [[Super League]] club [[Warrington Wolves]] for an undisclosed fee.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/warrington-confirm-signing-of-matty-ashton/|title=Warrington confirm signing of Matty Ashton|website=www.loverugbyleague.com|accessdate=17 Dec 2020}}</ref> He made his Super League début for Warrington against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in January 2020.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match/EVP3214858|title=Super League: Wigan Warriors v Warrington Wolves|publisher=BBC}}</ref>

In round 5 of the 2023 Super League season, Ashton scored a [[hat-trick]] in Warrington's 38–20 victory over [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/64953363#:~:text=Matty%20Ashton's%20hat%2Dtrick%20ensured,Josh%20Charnley%20scores%20for%20Leigh./|title=Super League: Warrington Wolves 38-20 Leigh Leopards - Ashton treble as Wire win again|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
Ashton played 25 games for Warrington in the 2023 Super League season and scored 18 tries as Warrington finished sixth on the table and qualified for the playoffs. Ashton played in the clubs elimination playoff loss against St Helens.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/23827325.highs-lows-warrington-wolves-2023-season/|title=Highs and lows from Warrington Wolves' 2023 season|website=www.warringtonguardian.co.uk}}</ref>
On 8 June 2024, Ashton played in Warrington's [[2024 Challenge Cup final]] defeat against Wigan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c2jj1x7m71zo|title=Wigan beat Warrington to win Challenge Cup|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
Ashton played a total of 25 games for Warrington in the 2024 Super League season and scored 27 tries as the club reached the semi-final before losing to Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/24654682.warrington-wolves-2024-season-review-february-march/|title=Warrington Wolves 2024 season review, February and March|website=www.warringtonguardian.co.uk}}</ref>

==International career==
On 28 October 2023 he scored two tries and won [[Player of the match]] in his second game for {{rlnt|England}} v {{rlnt|Tonga}}.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/no-helmets-required/2023/nov/06/england-tonga-series-world-cup-huddersfield-st-helens-leeds/|title=England's 3-0 win over Tonga shows why they are the third best team|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>
